summary: CNN《今晨》节目讨论特朗普与伊朗签署的结束战争协议。共和党人批评该协议是“美国投降”和“外交政策失误”，认为伊朗更强而盟友更弱。协议允许伊朗保留60%浓缩铀并在当地降级至3.67%，而非像2015年协议那样运出。以色列要求零浓缩，但协议未实现。特朗普在Truth Social上反击批评者。节目还涉及纽约市长确认泰勒和特拉维斯婚礼日期、华盛顿特区市长初选社会主义者领先等话题。

vocabulary.en: a stupid or careless mistake
vocabulary.excerpt: This will go down as a tremendous foreign policy blunder.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这将被视为一次巨大的外交政策失误。

vocabulary.en: the act of giving up or yielding to another
vocabulary.excerpt: some calling it an American surrender
vocabulary.example_cn: 一些人称其为美国的投降。

vocabulary.cn: （核材料）降级混合，降低浓缩度
vocabulary.en: to reduce the enrichment level of uranium by mixing with lower-enriched material
vocabulary.excerpt: will be down-blended locally
vocabulary.example_cn: 将在当地进行降级混合。

vocabulary.en: a large store of goods or materials kept for future use
vocabulary.excerpt: the stockpile, which at this point is at 60 percent
vocabulary.example_cn: 目前浓缩度达60%的库存。

vocabulary.en: uranium with a higher concentration of the isotope U-235, used for nuclear power or weapons
vocabulary.excerpt: Iran had about, it says, 20 percent enriched.
vocabulary.example_cn: 伊朗拥有约20%的浓缩铀。

vocabulary.en: the rapid increase or spread of something, especially nuclear weapons
vocabulary.excerpt: mitigate the concerns for proliferation
vocabulary.example_cn: 缓解对核扩散的担忧。

vocabulary.cn: 《不扩散核武器条约》（Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty）
vocabulary.en: Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons
vocabulary.excerpt: the legal interpretation of the NPT
vocabulary.example_cn: 对《不扩散核武器条约》的法律解释。

vocabulary.cn: 《联合全面行动计划》（Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action），即2015年伊朗核协议
vocabulary.en: Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action
vocabulary.excerpt: what the JCPOA stipulated was that Iran would ship out 98 percent of its enriched uranium
vocabulary.example_cn: JCPOA规定伊朗将运出98%的浓缩铀。

vocabulary.en: a document describing a formal agreement between parties
vocabulary.excerpt: this particular framework, this particular MOU
vocabulary.example_cn: 这一特定框架，这一谅解备忘录。

vocabulary.en: to reduce the effectiveness or strength of something
vocabulary.excerpt: The military in Iran has been degraded
vocabulary.example_cn: 伊朗的军事力量已被削弱。

vocabulary.en: a missile that follows a ballistic trajectory
vocabulary.excerpt: The ballistic missile stockpile has been degraded
vocabulary.example_cn: 弹道导弹库存已被削弱。

vocabulary.en: to complain or protest loudly
vocabulary.excerpt: why would all these conservative voices be squawking about this?
vocabulary.example_cn: 为什么所有这些保守派声音都在大声抱怨？

vocabulary.en: to affect the most important part of something
vocabulary.excerpt: this strikes at the very heart of the issue for them
vocabulary.example_cn: 这触及了他们问题的核心。

vocabulary.cn: 达到紧要关头，到了必须解决的地步
vocabulary.en: to reach a critical point where a decision or action is needed
vocabulary.excerpt: This all kind of comes to a head now
vocabulary.example_cn: 这一切现在到了紧要关头。

vocabulary.en: to last longer than someone or something
vocabulary.excerpt: know that they will outlast President Trump
vocabulary.example_cn: 知道他们会比特朗普总统更持久。

vocabulary.cn: 特朗普创立的社交媒体平台
vocabulary.en: a social media platform founded by Donald Trump
vocabulary.excerpt: your favorite person just posted to Truth Social
vocabulary.example_cn: 你最喜欢的人刚刚在Truth Social上发帖。

vocabulary.en: to fall rapidly in value or amount
vocabulary.excerpt: Oil prices are 'tumbling' down
vocabulary.example_cn: 油价正在暴跌。

vocabulary.en: a basic structure underlying a system or concept
vocabulary.excerpt: this is just a framework. This isn't the total deal.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这只是一个框架，不是完整的协议。

vocabulary.cn: 扫兴的人或事，破坏气氛者
vocabulary.en: something that spoils the mood or atmosphere
vocabulary.excerpt: World Cup fans are booing water breaks as a vibe killer
vocabulary.example_cn: 世界杯球迷嘘补水暂停，认为它破坏气氛。

vocabulary.en: completely wrong
vocabulary.excerpt: So, is FIFA all wet?
vocabulary.example_cn: 那么，国际足联完全错了吗？

vocabulary.cn: 在虚线上签字，正式签署协议
vocabulary.en: to formally agree to something by signing a contract
vocabulary.excerpt: So, signed on the dotted line.
vocabulary.example_cn: 所以，正式签署了协议。

vocabulary.en: to be remembered or recorded as something
vocabulary.excerpt: This will go down as a tremendous foreign policy blunder.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这将被视为一次巨大的外交政策失误。

vocabulary.en: to block or restrict something, especially a route
vocabulary.excerpt: if they're willing to grab that Strait of Hormuz and choke it off
vocabulary.example_cn: 如果他们愿意抓住霍尔木兹海峡并封锁它。

vocabulary.cn: 听命于某人，按某人的意愿行事
vocabulary.en: to do what someone wants you to do
vocabulary.excerpt: they can get the Western world to dance to their tune
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们可以让西方世界听命于他们。

sentences.en: Now that we've all seen the agreement President Trump signed with Iran, you have to ask, was it worth going to war over?
sentences.cn: 既然我们都看到了特朗普总统与伊朗签署的协议，你不得不问，这值得为之开战吗？
sentences.structure: 主句为'you have to ask'，前面是'now that'引导的原因状语从句，后面是直接引语疑问句作宾语。
sentences.analysis: now that 引导原因状语从句，表示'既然'；was it worth going to war over 是省略了 that 的宾语从句，over 置于句末。

sentences.en: This will go down as a tremendous foreign policy blunder.
sentences.cn: 这将被视为一个巨大的外交政策失误。
sentences.structure: 简单主谓宾结构，'go down as' 是固定短语作谓语。
sentences.analysis: go down as 意为'被记录为/被视为'，常用于新闻评论；blunder 指'愚蠢的错误'。

sentences.en: Conservative voices online, not pleased about what's in the text of this agreement, some calling it an American surrender, saying it's even worse than the Obama deal of 2015.
sentences.cn: 网上的保守派声音，对协议文本内容不满，有些人称其为美国的投降，说它甚至比2015年的奥巴马协议更糟糕。
sentences.structure: 主句为'Conservative voices online'，后接两个并列的现在分词短语'not pleased about...'和'some calling...'作伴随状语，'saying...'是第二个分词短语中的进一步说明。
sentences.analysis: not pleased about 是形容词短语作状语；calling it...和 saying...是现在分词短语，表示伴随动作；deal 在此指'协议'。

sentences.en: Senator Ted Cruz told 'The Daily Wire' he felt like Trump was getting very poor advice and called it ill-advised.
sentences.cn: 参议员特德·克鲁兹告诉《每日电讯》，他觉得特朗普得到了非常糟糕的建议，并称其不明智。
sentences.structure: 主句为'Senator Ted Cruz told...'，后接省略 that 的宾语从句'he felt like...'，其中包含并列谓语'felt like'和'called'。
sentences.analysis: felt like 后接从句，意为'感觉好像'；ill-advised 是形容词，意为'不明智的'。

sentences.en: Iran ends up stronger, our allies in the region are weaker, and Iran has learned that, if they're willing to grab that Strait of Hormuz and choke it off, they can get the Western world to dance to their tune.
sentences.cn: 伊朗最终变得更强大，我们在该地区的盟友更弱，而且伊朗已经学到，如果他们愿意抓住霍尔木兹海峡并掐断它，他们就能让西方世界听命于他们。
sentences.structure: 三个并列分句：第一个是主系表，第二个是主系表，第三个是主谓宾，宾语从句中包含 if 条件状语从句。
sentences.analysis: ends up 意为'最终变得'；choke off 意为'掐断/封锁'；dance to their tune 是习语，意为'听其摆布'。

sentences.en: In the Obama deal, basically, the plan was you ship out all your enriched uranium, right?
sentences.cn: 在奥巴马协议中，基本上，计划是你把所有浓缩铀运出去，对吧？
sentences.structure: 主句为'the plan was'，后接表语从句'you ship out...'，'basically'是插入语。
sentences.analysis: ship out 意为'运出'；enriched uranium 指'浓缩铀'；right 是口语中的确认问句。

sentences.en: But in this deal, it's a little different: basically, the stockpile, which at this point is at 60 percent, is kept inside Iran, and then will be down-blended locally.
sentences.cn: 但在这个协议中，情况有点不同：基本上，库存——目前达到60%——被保留在伊朗境内，然后将在当地进行降级混合。
sentences.structure: 主句为'it's a little different'，冒号后是解释说明，主语'the stockpile'后接非限制性定语从句'which...'，谓语是'is kept'和'will be down-blended'并列。
sentences.analysis: stockpile 指'库存'；down-blended 是专业术语，意为'降级混合'（降低浓缩度）；locally 意为'在当地'。

sentences.en: What the JCPOA stipulated was that Iran would ship out 98 percent of its enriched uranium to Russia.
sentences.cn: 《联合全面行动计划》规定，伊朗将把98%的浓缩铀运往俄罗斯。
sentences.structure: 主语从句'What the JCPOA stipulated'作主语，系动词was，后接表语从句'that Iran would ship out...'。
sentences.analysis: JCPOA 是 Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action 的缩写，即《联合全面行动计划》；stipulated 意为'规定'；ship out 意为'运出'。

sentences.en: Iran's interpretation, or the legal interpretation of the NPT, the article four of the NPT, their interpretation is that that right to use peaceful energy includes enrichment.
sentences.cn: 伊朗的解释，或者说对《不扩散核武器条约》的法律解释，该条约第四条，他们的解释是，使用和平能源的权利包括浓缩。
sentences.structure: 主句为'their interpretation is that...'，前面是同位语'Iran's interpretation, or the legal interpretation...'，表语从句中'that right...'作主语。
sentences.analysis: NPT 是 Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty 的缩写；article four 指第四条；enrichment 指'铀浓缩'。

sentences.en: What President Obama did was that he said the enrichment in Iran is a fact on the ground.
sentences.cn: 奥巴马总统所做的是，他说伊朗的浓缩是一个既成事实。
sentences.structure: 主语从句'What President Obama did'作主语，系动词was，后接表语从句'that he said...'，其中'said'后接省略 that 的宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: a fact on the ground 是固定表达，意为'既成事实/实际情况'。

sentences.en: So, the reason why I wanted to set the table with all that is, like, why would all these conservative voices be squawking about this?
sentences.cn: 所以，我想用所有这些来铺垫的原因是，为什么所有这些保守派声音都在对此嚷嚷？
sentences.structure: 主句为'the reason why...is'，后接表语从句'why would...'，'like'是插入语。
sentences.analysis: set the table 本义是'摆桌子'，这里比喻'做铺垫'；squawking about 是口语，意为'大声抱怨/嚷嚷'。

sentences.en: Why would the Lindsey Grahams of the world, who were all too happy to be a part of these strikes and have these demands on Iran, be so frustrated with what was signed?
sentences.cn: 为什么世界上的林赛·格雷厄姆们，他们非常乐意参与这些打击并对伊朗提出这些要求，却对所签署的东西如此沮丧？
sentences.structure: 主句为'Why would the Lindsey Grahams...be so frustrated...'，中间插入非限制性定语从句'who were all too happy...'，介词'with'后接宾语从句'what was signed'。
sentences.analysis: the Lindsey Grahams of the world 是借代用法，指像林赛·格雷厄姆那样的人；all too happy 意为'非常乐意'；frustrated with 意为'对...感到沮丧'。

sentences.en: This all kind of comes to a head now, because this deal and what's emerging, by all accounts, in the view of these Republicans, didn't accomplish that goal.
sentences.cn: 这一切现在都到了紧要关头，因为根据所有说法，在这些共和党人看来，这个协议以及正在出现的情况并没有实现那个目标。
sentences.structure: 主句为'This all kind of comes to a head now'，后接 because 原因状语从句，从句主语是'this deal and what's emerging'，中间插入'by all accounts'和'in the view of these Republicans'。
sentences.analysis: comes to a head 是习语，意为'到了紧要关头/达到顶点'；by all accounts 意为'根据各方面说法'；in the view of 意为'在...看来'。

sentences.en: And so, for these Republicans who, you know, see this as a deeply held principle for them and know that they will outlast President Trump, they're willing to speak up about it now.
sentences.cn: 因此，对于这些共和党人来说，他们视此为根深蒂固的原则，并且知道他们会比特朗普总统更长久，他们现在愿意大声说出来。
sentences.structure: 主句为'they're willing to speak up about it now'，前面是介词短语'for these Republicans'，后接定语从句'who...see...and know...'，其中'know'后接宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: deeply held principle 意为'根深蒂固的原则'；outlast 意为'比...活得长/比...持久'；speak up 意为'大声说出/直言不讳'。

sentences.en: President Trump says, look, 'These fools who think I haven't been tough enough on Iran when the Stock Market Just Hit a RECORD HIGH, and Oil prices are 'tumbling' down, are either jealous, bad people or stupid.'
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统说，看，'这些傻瓜，他们认为我对伊朗不够强硬，而股市刚刚创下历史新高，油价正在暴跌，他们要么是嫉妒，要么是坏人，要么是愚蠢。'
sentences.structure: 主句为'President Trump says'，后接直接引语，引语中主句为'These fools...are either jealous, bad people or stupid'，中间插入定语从句'who think...'，定语从句中包含 when 时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: tough enough on 意为'对...足够强硬'；RECORD HIGH 全大写表示强调；tumbling down 意为'暴跌'；either...or... 连接并列表语。

sentences.en: The military in Iran has been degraded, whether or not it's been blasted to the Stone Age.
sentences.cn: 伊朗的军事力量已被削弱，无论是否被炸回石器时代。
sentences.structure: 主句为'The military...has been degraded'，后接 whether or not 引导的让步状语从句。
sentences.analysis: degraded 意为'削弱/降级'；blasted to the Stone Age 是夸张说法，意为'炸回石器时代'。

sentences.en: We spent $1 billion a day fighting a war that no one wanted to go to.
sentences.cn: 我们每天花费10亿美元打一场没人想打的战争。
sentences.structure: 主句为'We spent $1 billion a day'，后接现在分词短语'fighting a war'作状语，其中包含定语从句'that no one wanted to go to'修饰'war'。
sentences.analysis: spend...doing 结构；go to war 是固定短语，意为'参战'，定语从句中 to 置于句末。

sentences.en: But a lot of people say, why should Iran have almost immediate access to investments in the world economy if it's still sitting on its enriched uranium and so much of this is not worked out yet?
sentences.cn: 但很多人说，如果伊朗仍然持有其浓缩铀，而且这么多问题尚未解决，为什么它应该几乎立即获得世界经济的投资？
sentences.structure: 主句为'a lot of people say'，后接直接引语疑问句，疑问句中包含 if 条件状语从句，从句中并列两个分句。
sentences.analysis: have access to 意为'获得/使用'；sitting on 意为'持有/拥有'；work out 意为'解决/制定出'。

topics.title: 美伊新协议与2015年JCPOA对比
topics.content: 2026年美伊协议允许伊朗保留60%浓缩铀并在当地降级至3.67%，而2015年JCPOA要求伊朗将98%浓缩铀运往俄罗斯。新协议被批评为“美国投降”，共和党人认为伊朗更强而盟友更弱。以色列要求零浓缩，但协议未实现。
topics.keywords: JCPOA · 浓缩铀 · 降级混合 · 零浓缩

topics.title: 共和党内部对特朗普伊朗政策的批评
topics.content: 共和党参议员Bill Cassidy称协议为“外交政策失误”，Nikki Haley称其为“巨大错误”，Ted Cruz批评特朗普接受糟糕建议。特朗普在Truth Social上反击，称批评者“嫉妒、坏人或愚蠢”。
topics.keywords: 外交政策失误 · 美国投降 · Truth Social · 共和党

topics.title: 伊朗核问题中的法律争议：NPT与浓缩权
topics.content: 伊朗依据《不扩散核武器条约》（NPT）第四条主张和平利用核能包括浓缩权，美国否认。这一法律解释分歧持续20多年，导致协议难以达成零浓缩目标。
topics.keywords: NPT · 浓缩权 · 和平利用核能 · 法律解释

topics.title: 协议的经济与安全影响
topics.content: 协议设立3000亿美元投资基金，用于稳定伊朗经济。批评者认为伊朗在保留浓缩铀的情况下不应获得资金。支持者认为稳定伊朗对地区安全至关重要。
topics.keywords: 投资基金 · 经济稳定 · 地区安全 · 3000亿美元

quiz.question: 原文中 'This will go down as a tremendous foreign policy blunder.' 中 'blunder' 最接近的意思是？
quiz.explanation: blunder 意为重大错误或失误，与上下文批评协议一致。

quiz.question: 原文 'will be down-blended locally' 中 'down-blend' 指的是？
quiz.explanation: down-blend 指将高浓缩铀与低浓缩铀混合以降低其浓缩度，减少武器化风险。

quiz.question: 原文 'Iran ends up stronger. Our allies in the region are weaker' 中 'ends up' 的含义是？
quiz.explanation: ends up 表示最终处于某种状态，此处指伊朗最终变得更强大。

quiz.question: 根据原文，共和党人批评新协议的主要原因是什么？
options: A. 协议没有结束战争
B. 协议让伊朗保留浓缩铀且盟友变弱
C. 协议花费太多美国资金
D. 协议没有经过国会批准
quiz.explanation: 原文中Cassidy说'伊朗更强，盟友更弱'，且协议允许伊朗保留浓缩铀，这是批评核心。

quiz.question: 特朗普在Truth Social上如何回应批评？
options: B. 指责批评者嫉妒、坏人或愚蠢
quiz.explanation: 原文中特朗普称批评者为'嫉妒、坏人或愚蠢'，并强调股市新高和油价下跌。

quiz.question: 关于新协议与2015年JCPOA的区别，以下哪项正确？
options: A. 新协议要求伊朗运出所有浓缩铀
B. 新协议允许伊朗保留浓缩铀并在当地降级
C. 新协议禁止伊朗任何浓缩活动
D. 新协议与JCPOA完全相同
quiz.explanation: 原文指出JCPOA要求运出98%浓缩铀，而新协议允许保留并在当地降级至3.67%。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.text: Now that we've all seen the agreement President Trump signed with Iran, you have to ask, was it worth going to war over? SEN. BILL CASSIDY (R-LA): This will go down as a tremendous foreign policy blunder.
paragraphs.cn: 既然我们都看到了特朗普总统与伊朗签署的协议，你不得不问，这值得为此开战吗？参议员比尔·卡西迪（路易斯安那州共和党人）：这将被视为一个巨大的外交政策失误。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And Vice President Vance better look out. The bus could be coming around the corner.
paragraphs.cn: 万斯副总统最好小心点。巴士可能正从拐角处驶来。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: This way, if it works out, I'm going to take the credit. If it doesn't work out, I'm blaming J.D. You better be careful,
paragraphs.cn: 这样一来，如果成功了，我会居功。如果失败了，我就怪J.D.。你最好小心点。

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Cornish
paragraphs.text: And the world champion New York Knicks get an invite to the White House. But will any of the players boycott? In the meantime, World Cup fans are booing water breaks as a vibe killer that makes the games too -- I don't know -- American. So, is FIFA all wet?
paragraphs.cn: 世界冠军纽约尼克斯队受邀前往白宫。但会有球员抵制吗？与此同时，世界杯球迷们对补水暂停发出嘘声，认为这破坏了比赛节奏，让比赛变得太 --  -- 怎么说呢 --  -- 美国化。那么，国际足联是不是全湿了？

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.text: This was not easy, I can tell you.
paragraphs.cn: 我可以告诉你，这并不容易。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, signed on the dotted line. The U.S. and Iran have both signed an agreement to end the war on all fronts, for better or worse, at least for the U.S. Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to start with the details of that agreement, but also the Republican response. Conservative voices online, not pleased about what's in the text of this agreement, some calling it an American surrender, saying it's even worse than the Obama deal of 2015.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，在虚线上签了字。美国和伊朗都签署了一项协议，以结束所有战线上的战争，无论好坏，至少对美国来说是这样。大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什。我们将从该协议的细节开始，但也会关注共和党的反应。网上的保守派声音对协议文本的内容并不满意，有些人称其为美国的投降，甚至比2015年的奥巴马协议还要糟糕。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, Nikki Haley called it a, quote, "huge mistake to pay to rebuild the threat we just destroyed." Senator Ted Cruz told "The Daily Wire" he felt like Trump was getting very poor advice and called it ill-advised. In the meantime, Senator Bill Cassidy took it one step further.
paragraphs.cn: 妮基·黑利称其为"花钱重建我们刚刚摧毁的威胁的巨大错误"。参议员特德·克鲁兹告诉《每日电讯报》，他觉得特朗普得到了非常糟糕的建议，并称其不明智。与此同时，参议员比尔·卡西迪更进一步。

paragraphs.speaker: Cassidy
paragraphs.text: This will go down as a tremendous foreign policy blunder. Iran ends up stronger. Our allies in the region are weaker, and Iran has learned that, if they're willing to grab that Strait of Hormuz and choke it off, they can get the Western world to dance to their tune. I think it's a deep mistake.
paragraphs.cn: 这将被视为一个巨大的外交政策失误。伊朗最终变得更强大。我们在该地区的盟友更弱了，而伊朗已经学到，如果他们愿意抓住霍尔木兹海峡并扼住它，他们就能让西方世界跟着他们的节奏跳舞。我认为这是一个严重的错误。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Today in the group chat, Nicholas Wu, congressional reporter at "Semafor"; Sina Azodi, director of Middle East studies programs at George Washington University; Mike Dubke, former Trump White House communications director; and Meghan Hays, former Biden White House director of message planning. So, I want to start with you, Sina, because you've been watching this very closely. And one point interested me.
paragraphs.cn: 今天在群聊中，有《Semafor》的国会记者尼古拉斯·吴；乔治华盛顿大学中东研究项目主任西纳·阿佐迪；前特朗普白宫通讯主任迈克·杜布克；以及前拜登白宫信息规划主任梅根·海斯。所以，我想先问你，西纳，因为你一直在密切关注此事。有一点让我很感兴趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: In the Obama deal, basically, the plan was you ship out all your enriched uranium, right? Iran had about, it says, 20 percent enriched. And they would send that somewhere. It ended up being Russia. But in this deal -- in this deal, it's a little different. Basically, the stockpile, which at this point is at 60 percent, is kept inside Iran, and then will be down-blended locally. But it means they keep it on their soil. Is this one of the aspects of the deal that has people really upset?
paragraphs.cn: 在奥巴马协议中，基本上计划是你把所有浓缩铀运出去，对吧？伊朗当时有大约20%的浓缩铀。他们会将其送到某个地方，最终是俄罗斯。但在这个协议中 --  -- 在这个协议中，情况有点不同。基本上，目前达到60%的库存留在伊朗境内，然后在当地进行降级混合。但这意味着他们将其保留在自己的土地上。这是协议中让人们非常不满的方面之一吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Sina Azodi
paragraphs.text: Well, what the JCPOA stipulated was that Iran would ship out 98 percent of its enriched uranium to Russia. Right.
paragraphs.cn: 《联合全面行动计划》规定，伊朗将把其98%的浓缩铀运往俄罗斯。没错。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It leaves the country.
paragraphs.cn: 这些浓缩铀将离开伊朗。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.text: Leaves the country. In this case, I think for whatever reason -- it's distrust, the possibility of the U.S. leaving the deal again -- Iranians have insisted that it has to stay on their soil. But to, let's say, mitigate the concerns for proliferation, they're going to down-blend it to 3.67 percent, which is low enriched uranium. And you use that for a nuclear power plant.
paragraphs.cn: 离开伊朗。但在目前情况下，我认为出于某种原因 --  -- 不信任、美国可能再次退出协议 --  -- 伊朗坚持这些浓缩铀必须留在其境内。但为了缓解对核扩散的担忧，他们将把浓缩铀的丰度降低到3.67%，即低浓缩铀，用于核电站。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.text: Doesn't have any weaponization concerns.
paragraphs.cn: 这不存在武器化的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, but different from one leaves. One stays, basically.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，但这与离开伊朗不同。基本上，这些浓缩铀留在了伊朗。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And the administration is saying, look, this -- this is a good solution, because it's going to get us to zero. But it leads to my other point. It's not -- it's non-zero, not zero.
paragraphs.cn: 而美国政府表示，这是一个好的解决方案，因为它将使我们达到零。但这引出了我的另一个观点。这不是零，而是非零。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And I know Israel said, look, there should be zero. No enrichment whatsoever. Nothing enriched anywhere. But that is not what this deal does.
paragraphs.cn: 我知道以色列说过，应该是零。不允许任何浓缩活动。任何地方都不能有浓缩铀。但这项协议并没有做到这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.text: No. And the problem -- and we've been dealing with this issue of the principal of enrichment in Iran for more than 20 years now. Iran's interpretation, or the legal interpretation of the NPT, the article four of the NPT, their interpretation is that that right to use peaceful energy includes enrichment. The U.S. side says, no, absolutely not. The text of the NPT doesn't say enrichment. So, they've been fighting over this on this legal interpretation.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。问题在于 --  -- 我们处理伊朗浓缩铀原则问题已经超过20年了。伊朗对《不扩散核武器条约》第四条的解释是，和平利用核能的权利包括浓缩铀。而美方则坚决反对，因为条约文本并未提及浓缩铀。因此，双方一直就这一法律解释争论不休。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And that fight hasn't changed? Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 这场争论没有改变？是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.text: Hasn't changed -- changed. What President Obama did was that he said the enrichment in Iran is a fact on the ground. We can't change it. President Trump said, no, it's a bad deal. Withdrew. And we are back to 2018 now.
paragraphs.cn: 没有改变。奥巴马总统的做法是承认伊朗的浓缩铀是既成事实，我们无法改变。特朗普总统则认为这是一项糟糕的协议，并退出了。现在我们又回到了2018年的状态。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. So, the reason why I wanted to set the table with all that is, like, why would all these conservative voices be squawking about this? Right? Why would the Lindsey Grahams of the world, who were all too happy to be a part of these strikes and have these demands on Iran, be so frustrated with what was signed? Nicholas, what's your sense in Congress about, A, why they feel comfortable saying it out loud and why they're saying what they're saying? NICHOLAS WU, CONGRESSIONAL REPORTER, "SEMAFOR": Well, for
paragraphs.cn: 是的。所以，我想摆出所有这些事实的原因是，为什么这些保守派声音会对此大惊小怪？对吧？为什么像林赛·格雷厄姆这样的人，他们曾非常乐意参与这些打击行动并对伊朗提出这些要求，现在却对签署的协议如此不满？尼古拉斯，你在国会的感觉如何？第一，为什么他们觉得可以公开说出来；第二，他们为什么这么说？《Semafor》国会记者尼古拉斯·吴：嗯，对于

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Republicans on Capitol Hill that I've talked to, I mean, this strikes at the very heart of the issue for them; like, the issue of Iran's nuclear program, the reasons they went to war. This all kind of comes to a head now, because this deal and what's emerging, by all accounts, in the view of these Republicans, didn't accomplish that goal.
paragraphs.cn: 我交谈过的国会山共和党人来说，这触及了他们问题的核心；比如，伊朗核计划问题，他们发动战争的原因。现在这一切都到了紧要关头，因为根据所有说法，在这群共和党人看来，这项协议及其产生的结果并没有实现那个目标。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And so, for these Republicans who, you know, see this as a deeply held principle for them and know that they will outlast President Trump, they're willing to speak up about it now.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，对于这些共和党人来说，他们视此为一项根深蒂固的原则，并且知道他们会比特朗普总统更长久，所以他们现在愿意直言不讳。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. It's interesting. As we are speaking, Mike Dubke --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。这很有趣。就在我们说话的时候，迈克·杜布克 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- your favorite person just posted to Truth Social. President Trump says, look, "These fools who think I haven't been tough enough on Iran when the Stock Market Just Hit a RECORD HIGH, and Oil prices are 'tumbling' down, are either jealous, bad people or stupid." So, let's talk messaging.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 你最喜欢的人刚刚在Truth Social上发帖。特朗普总统说，看，"这些傻瓜认为我对伊朗不够强硬，而股市刚刚创下历史新高，油价正在'暴跌'，他们要么是嫉妒，要么是坏人，要么是愚蠢。"那么，我们来谈谈信息传递。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Well, first of all, this is just a framework. This isn't the total deal. So, we need to keep that in mind. I mean, we keep -- all the rhetoric I keep hearing is, you know, this deal, this deal, that. It's a framework to get to a deal.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，首先，这只是一个框架。这不是完整的协议。所以，我们需要记住这一点。我的意思是，我们一直 --  -- 我一直听到的所有言论都是，你知道，这个协议，那个协议。这只是达成协议的一个框架。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: So, we can argue whether or not that's great or not. And I appreciate the -- the laughter on my left. The other thing is when you're in a hole, what's the first rule? Stop digging. And I think that's where we are with this particular framework, this particular MOU, is that we are at a point. The military in Iran has been degraded, whether or not it's been blasted to the Stone Age. Again, we can debate that. The ballistic missile stockpile has been degraded. Their ability to fund Hamas, Hezbollah and the Houthis --
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我们可以争论这是好是坏。我感谢我左边的笑声。另一件事是，当你陷入困境时，第一条规则是什么？停止挖坑。我认为这就是我们在这个特定框架、这个特定谅解备忘录上的处境，我们正处于一个节点。伊朗的军事力量已经被削弱，无论是否被炸回石器时代。再说一次，我们可以辩论。弹道导弹库存已被削弱。他们资助哈马斯、真主党和胡塞武装的能力 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And we should say --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我们应该说 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: -- has been degraded.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 已经被削弱了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- Europe, NATO. Marc Rich (ph) was speaking earlier. I don't know if that was sound. But they're not saying, Oh, God, this is really bad. Right? We heard Carney yesterday say, I like what I saw.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 欧洲、北约。马克·里奇早些时候在发言。我不知道那是不是声音。但他们并没有说，哦，天哪，这真的很糟糕。对吧？我们昨天听到卡尼说，我喜欢我所看到的。

paragraphs.speaker: Meghan Hays
paragraphs.text: Well, what are they supposed to do? They're standing with him. They're not going to say anything in front of him. First of all, we spent $1 billion a day fighting a war that no one wanted to go to. So, we spent over $100 billion there. We spent $300 billion in this deal. So, now we're $400 billion when Americans don't have healthcare.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，他们应该怎么做？他们支持他。他们不会在他面前说什么。首先，我们每天花费10亿美元打一场没人想打的战争。我们在那里花费了超过1000亿美元。我们在这笔交易中花费了3000亿美元。所以，现在我们已经花了4000亿美元，而美国人却没有医疗保障。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: We did not spend $300 billion in this deal. If --
paragraphs.cn: 我们在这笔交易中并没有花费3000亿美元。如果 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: You tell me, then --
paragraphs.cn: 那你告诉我 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: I just can't lay that. I just can't let you just get away with that.
paragraphs.cn: 我不能就这样放过你。我不能让你就这么蒙混过关。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Let me give the fact check. There will be a $300 billion --
paragraphs.cn: 好的。让我来核实一下事实。将会有3000亿美元 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- investment fund. But a lot of people say, why should Iran have almost immediate access to investments in the world economy if it's still sitting on its enriched uranium and so much of this is not worked out yet?
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 的投资基金。但很多人说，如果伊朗仍然持有浓缩铀，而且很多问题尚未解决，为什么伊朗应该几乎立即获得对世界经济的投资？

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.text: I would say that a possibility of instability in Iran is a danger to everybody, the entire region.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为伊朗的不稳定可能对所有人、整个地区构成危险。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, you think the money is necessary to restabilize?
paragraphs.cn: 所以，你认为这笔钱对于重新稳定是必要的？

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.text: To restabilize the country. And I think that it -- you know, just looking at President Trump's approach, investment, it could work for the Americans, too. A lot of jobs. Think of it: in 2015, Iran signed an agreement with Boeing to buy passenger planes, which never happened, because President Trump decided to leave.
paragraphs.cn: 为了重新稳定这个国家。而且我认为，你知道，看看特朗普总统的做法，投资也可能对美国有利。很多就业机会。想想看：2015年，伊朗与波音公司签署了购买客机的协议，但从未实现，因为特朗普总统决定退出。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But you can hear why critics would say, Well, wait a second. Now were talking about investment with Iran, with a regime that fundamentally is not changed.
paragraphs.cn: 但你可以理解为什么批评者会说，等等，现在我们谈论的是与伊朗投资，与一个根本未改变的政权。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: Exactly my point. So now, Americans are using -- the American taxpayers are paying for something that we never -- a war we didn't want to go to. Gas prices shot up, and got a huge mess to our global economy, not to mention our own economy here. And Trump doesn't seem to care. He just is like, Oh, if you're stupid, you don't like this. Well, they still have -- they still have the capability for nuclear power -- or nuclear war capabilities.
paragraphs.cn: 这正是我的观点。所以现在，美国人正在用 --  -- 美国纳税人在为我们从未想要参与的战争买单。油价飙升，给全球经济造成了巨大混乱，更不用说我们自己的经济了。而特朗普似乎并不在乎。他就像在说，哦，如果你愚蠢，你就不会喜欢这个。好吧，他们仍然拥有核能 --  -- 或者说核战争的能力。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: This is an agreement to -- to a deal, which this is the 39th time he said that we almost have a deal. So, at least we're one step closer there. But I just don't understand how we are in this position that -- in a war that no one wanted to go to, and the American people are the ones suffering.
paragraphs.cn: 这是一项协议 --  -- 一项交易，这是他第39次说我们快达成协议了。所以，至少我们离目标更近了一步。但我就是不明白，我们怎么会陷入这样一场没人想打的战争，而受苦的却是美国人民。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let me give Sina the last word, because he's here. And this was signed at Versailles. Lots of things have been signed at Versailles.
paragraphs.cn: 让我给西娜最后发言的机会，因为他在这里。这是在凡尔赛签署的。很多协议都是在凡尔赛签署的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But usually, when you call something a Versailles treaty, it's in foreign language policy land. Kind of an insult, right? It's a self-defeating agreement. What's your response to the critics out there who are making those analogies?
paragraphs.cn: 但通常，当你把某件事称为凡尔赛条约时，那是在外交政策领域。有点侮辱性，对吧？这是一项自毁性的协议。你对那些做出这种类比批评者有什么回应？

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.text: Look, I mean, absolutely what Meghan said, I mean, this was the -- President Trump didn't have to sign that peace deal at Versailles today. He could have had an agreement in February before he decided to go to war. He was dragged into a war of choice that he didn't have to. Thirteen Americans died. Billions of dollars spent. He could have taken the deal that the Iranians had offered, and it was a pretty good deal compared to the JCPOA. And I know that President Trump is --
paragraphs.cn: 听着，我完全同意梅根说的。特朗普总统今天不必在凡尔赛签署那份和平协议。他本可以在二月份决定开战之前就达成协议。他是被拖入一场本不必参与的选择性战争。13名美国人丧生，花费了数十亿美元。他本可以接受伊朗提出的协议，而且与《联合全面行动计划》相比，那是一个相当不错的协议。我知道特朗普总统 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Did not believe that.
paragraphs.cn: 不相信这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.text: -- very sensitive to the word "JCPOA" and Obama.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 对"JCPOA"和奥巴马这个词非常敏感。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.text: But that was a very good deal that he had on February 26 in Geneva.
paragraphs.cn: 但那是他2月26日在日内瓦得到的一个非常好的协议。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, the hope is -- from the White House that 60 days from now, whatever they have will be much better --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，白宫希望60天后，无论他们得到什么，都会比2015年发生的好得多 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- than happened in 2015. Thank you so much for your expertise. I hope you can come back.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 比2015年发生的好。非常感谢你的专业见解。希望你能再来。

paragraphs.speaker: Azodi
paragraphs.text: Thank you for having me.
paragraphs.cn: 感谢您的邀请。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Coming up on CNN THIS MORNING, there's a new release date for Sean "Diddy" Combs. What this means for his time behind bars. And Swifties, did New York's mayor just let a secret slip?
paragraphs.cn: 在《CNN今晨》节目中，肖恩·"吹牛老爹"·康布斯有了新的释放日期。这对他的牢狱生涯意味着什么。还有，斯威夫特们，纽约市长是不是不小心泄露了一个秘密？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Taylor Swift wedding confirmed by Mamdani, you guys.
paragraphs.cn: 马姆达尼证实了泰勒·斯威夫特的婚礼，各位。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It's now 14 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. A tornado threat looming over the Midwest today. This morning, more than 65,000 people are actually still without power in the region after severe storms last night. The National Weather Service reported several tornadoes and issued some of its highest-level tornado alerts. And Luigi Mangione's team will present a psychiatric defense in his state murder trial.
paragraphs.cn: 现在是整点过14分。这是您的早间新闻摘要。今天中西部地区面临龙卷风威胁。今早，该地区仍有超过6.5万人因昨晚的严重风暴而断电。美国国家气象局报告了多起龙卷风，并发布了最高级别的龙卷风警报。此外，路易吉·曼吉奥内的团队将在其州级谋杀案审判中提出精神障碍辩护。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: They're expected to admit he killed the UnitedHealthcare CEO, but under an extreme emotional disturbance. paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Crowd not sounding happy there. Telemundo says they want to create an authentic World Cup experience without cutting away from the action. So, joining me now to discuss, a retired professional soccer player, Alecko Eskandarian. Thank you so much for being here.
paragraphs.cn: 观众听起来不太高兴。Telemundo表示，他们希望打造真实的世界杯体验，不中断比赛画面。现在，请来一位退役职业足球运动员阿莱科·埃斯坎达里安与我讨论。非常感谢你的到来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, first, I want to talk about this being a new rule. And I gather it was to help deal with the heat. But I was listening to an after game press conference and I think it was the coach for the English national team, he said, look, this completely flattened our tempo and it gives the opposition a free tactical time-out to completely neutralize our press. Do water breaks kill momentum?
paragraphs.cn: 首先，我想谈谈这是一项新规则。我猜这是为了应对高温。但我听了赛后新闻发布会，好像是英格兰国家队的主教练说，这完全打乱了我们的节奏，给了对手一个免费的战术暂停，完全压制了我们的逼抢。那么，补水暂停会扼杀比赛势头吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Eskandarian
paragraphs.text: I don't think so. I mean, to be honest with you, yes, they are different and I think they were implemented, like you said, for player safety. I'd imagine for FIFA's -- from FIFA's purview, they established this to put in some consistency because there are going to be some games that will have it just because of the temperature and some that wouldn't. So, this was something that evens the playing field when it's involved in every game.
paragraphs.cn: 我不这么认为。说实话，是的，它们不同，而且我认为实施它们是为了球员安全。我想从国际足联的角度来看，他们设立这个是为了保持一致性，因为有些比赛会因为温度而有暂停，有些则没有。所以，当每场比赛都涉及暂停时，这能平衡比赛条件。

paragraphs.speaker: Eskandarian
paragraphs.text: And as long as every team knows that they're going to have that benefit, I think it evens it out.
paragraphs.cn: 只要每支球队都知道他们会受益，我认为这就能平衡。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, fans are booing. I assume players adapt. But I'm also thinking of some of those European tennis players, right, who were faced with the heat and people thought, why are they out there doing all this in the heat? Is there something the fans maybe aren't acknowledging about this experience?
paragraphs.cn: 所以，球迷们在嘘声。我猜球员们会适应。但我也想到一些欧洲网球选手，他们在高温下比赛，人们会想，为什么他们要在这种高温下比赛？球迷们是否没有意识到这种体验的某些方面？

paragraphs.speaker: Eskandarian
paragraphs.text: Yes, player health and safety, I would say. It's hot out there for sure. Definitely in some of the cities that the World Cup is taking place, it can definitely wear on you. So, I think player safety is at the -- at the forefront of this. And, yes, I don't think a 60 second break is really going to change tactics or player physicality that much that it's going to have any impact on the outcome of the game.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我认为是球员的健康和安全。那里确实很热。尤其是在一些举办世界杯的城市，高温确实会让人疲惫。所以，我认为球员安全是首要考虑。而且，我不认为60秒的暂停会真正改变战术或球员体能到影响比赛结果的程度。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: What top two games are you watching next?
paragraphs.cn: 接下来你最关注哪两场比赛？

paragraphs.speaker: Eskandarian
paragraphs.text: Oh, I'm going to be going out to L.A. for Belgium-Iran and USA-Turkey. So, I'm looking forward to watching those games in person. But to be honest with you, the tournament's been full of upsets, so every game is must watch.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，我会去洛杉矶看比利时对伊朗和美国对土耳其的比赛。所以，我很期待现场观看这些比赛。但说实话，这届锦标赛冷门频出，所以每场比赛都值得看。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. That's Alecko Eskandarian. Thank you so much for being here.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。这位是阿莱科·埃斯坎达里安。非常感谢你的到来。

paragraphs.speaker: Eskandarian
paragraphs.text: Thanks for having me.
paragraphs.cn: 感谢邀请。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And in the meantime, this Sunday, you can get an inside look at Team USA. Do they have what it takes to win the game's ultimate prize? We've got a flash doc "Chasing Soccer Glory: America's Long Game." That's airing Sunday night at 8 p.m. on CNN. You can also watch it on the CNN app. In Chicago today, the Obama Presidential Center is opening. It's been an emotional week leading up to it.
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，本周日，你可以深入了解美国队。他们是否有能力赢得比赛的最终大奖？我们有一部纪录片《追逐足球荣耀：美国的持久战》。该片将于周日晚8点在CNN播出。你也可以在CNN应用程序上观看。今天在芝加哥，奥巴马总统中心开幕。此前的一周令人情绪激动。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Former First Lady Michelle Obama surprised her husband at the reception ahead of the grand opening because she brought the former president to tears in this custom skirt, which bears the image honoring her late mother.
paragraphs.cn: 前第一夫人米歇尔·奥巴马在盛大开幕前的招待会上给了丈夫一个惊喜，她穿着这条定制裙子让前总统落泪，裙子上印有纪念她已故母亲的形象。

paragraphs.speaker: Michelle Obama
paragraphs.text: She was so proud of her son-in-law and the man that he is and has been to our family.
paragraphs.cn: 她为她的女婿以及他这个人、他对我们家庭的意义感到非常自豪。

paragraphs.speaker: Barack Obama
paragraphs.text: I am a little shaken up by this because I loved my mother-in-law.
paragraphs.cn: 我有点激动，因为我爱我的岳母。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, Obama has always been ingrained in pop culture. The event today is going to reflect that. The lineup of performers includes Stevie Wonder, U2's Bono, The Edge, Bruce Springsteen, Jennifer Hudson, The Roots, among others. And former presidents, Biden, Bush and Clinton are changing up their usual meeting spot, funerals, to be there for Obama. So, the group chat is back. I wanted to talk about this because all week President Trump has been referencing Obama.
paragraphs.cn: 如今，奥巴马一直深深植根于流行文化中。今天的活动将反映这一点。表演者阵容包括史蒂夫·旺德、U2的波诺、The Edge、布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀、詹妮弗·哈德森、The Roots等。前总统拜登、布什和克林顿改变了他们通常的会面地点 --  -- 葬礼，来为奥巴马捧场。所以，群聊又回来了。我想谈谈这个，因为整个星期特朗普总统都在提及奥巴马。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: He can't seem to stop no matter where this Iran deal is. And it occurred to me, between it being Juneteenth and this library opening, it is a week for people to be reminded of both Obama and his legacy. NICHOLAS WU, CONGRESSIONAL REPORTER, "SEMAFOR": That's for sure. And, I mean, I think what this library is doing is it, you know, this is a president's own telling of his legacy. And Obama is very much putting that out there.
paragraphs.cn: 他似乎无法停止，无论伊朗协议进展如何。我突然想到，在六月节和这个图书馆开幕之间，这一周让人们想起了奥巴马和他的遗产。国会记者尼古拉斯·吴（来自《Semafor》）：确实如此。而且，我认为这个图书馆所做的是，你知道，这是总统对自己遗产的讲述。奥巴马正在大力宣扬这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You know, we can debate as much as we want on how exactly -- what the legacy of the JCPOA was, and many other let's say his --
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，我们可以尽情争论《联合全面行动计划》的遗产到底是什么，以及许多其他 --  -- 比如他的 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, but his broader legacy, whether it be art that they're presenting there, basketball courts, community centers, this is Obama telling his story. WU: Exactly. And to me, the most interesting thing with this library is, you know, what do you do when all these records are not necessarily paper and, you know, like a traditional library.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，但他更广泛的遗产，无论是他们展示的艺术、篮球场、社区中心，这都是奥巴马在讲述他的故事。吴：没错。对我来说，这个图书馆最有趣的是，你知道，当所有这些记录不一定是纸质的时候，就像传统图书馆一样，你该怎么办。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Oh, right. WU: Like, how do you immortalize -- you know, when Obama was the cool president with a Blackberry and sending tweets, you know, how do you -- how do you do something like that? And so, I'm curious to see what they do with that.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，对。吴：比如，你如何使其不朽 --  -- 你知道，当奥巴马是拿着黑莓手机、发推文的酷总统时，你如何做到这一点？所以，我很想看看他们如何处理这个问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I also want to mention this event. It's very Obama era in terms of all the celebrities being there. And we made a list of, like, who's going to be -- who's going to be at this Obama event and their global record sales. And then what's going on with the Trump-backed Freedom 250, which prey much kicked off a few weeks ago in terms of who was leaving it. And in the end, it's Lee Greenwood, a president -- the president's favorite, and a handful of other artists.
paragraphs.cn: 我还想提一下这个活动。众多名人出席，这非常具有奥巴马时代的特征。我们列了一个清单，比如谁将参加这个奥巴马的活动，以及他们的全球唱片销量。然后还有特朗普支持的"自由250"活动，几周前刚刚启动，看看谁退出了。最后，是李·格林伍德，总统最喜爱的歌手，以及少数其他艺人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Is this a reminder of like kind of where Hollywood's heart lies or the complication that Trump has his relationship with pop culture.
paragraphs.cn: 这是否提醒我们好莱坞的立场所在，或者特朗普与流行文化关系的复杂性？

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: I don't know that it's a fair comparison to the -- I -- look, I love --
paragraphs.cn: 我不确定这是否是一个公平的比较 --  -- 我 --  -- 你看，我喜欢 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Because, here's the thing.
paragraphs.cn: 因为，事情是这样的。

paragraphs.speaker: I -- Cornish
paragraphs.text: They're two massive events.
paragraphs.cn: 这是两场大型活动。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: I love presidential libraries. I think they are, and especially to the point that they tell the story of the president in their own words. So, I've been to a few. I've been to Bill Clinton's down in Little Rock. I've been to Ronald Reagan's out in California. I think it's incredibly interesting to walk through each of these libraries. And I -- if I'm in Chicago, I will probably go to Obama's presidential library because I think they're fantastic things.
paragraphs.cn: 我喜欢总统图书馆。我认为它们很棒，尤其是它们用总统自己的话讲述总统的故事。我去过几个。我去过小石城的比尔·克林顿图书馆，也去过加利福尼亚的罗纳德·里根图书馆。我觉得参观这些图书馆非常有趣。如果我去芝加哥，我可能会去奥巴马的总统图书馆，因为我认为它们非常棒。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You would. Trump is not invited, just so we're clear.
paragraphs.cn: 你会去的。特朗普没有被邀请，我们得说清楚。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: I -- no, no, no, that's fine.
paragraphs.cn: 我 --  -- 不，不，不，没关系。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: But I don't -- I will take a little issue on this pop culture thing because I don't know that comparing America's 250 and all the politics that are surrounding this with what should be a celebration this week for Obama.
paragraphs.cn: 但我对流行文化这件事有点异议，因为我不确定将美国250周年以及围绕它的所有政治活动与本周本应是奥巴马庆祝活动的事情相提并论是否合适。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Well, Meghan, I'm going to --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。好吧，梅根，我要 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: I'm not -- I'm not sure on that.
paragraphs.cn: 我 --  -- 我不太确定这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Meghan Hays
paragraphs.text: So, Americans, we're not supposed to celebrate America's 250? I thought that was the whole point of this.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，美国人，我们不应该庆祝美国250周年吗？我以为这才是重点。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: No, no, I think we should be celebrating America's 250. But look, this is -- this is a celebration for Obama and the folks that really enjoyed his presidency. And, you know, good on them for having a good group.
paragraphs.cn: 不，不，我认为我们应该庆祝美国250周年。但你看，这是 --  -- 这是为奥巴马和那些真正享受他总统任期的人举办的庆祝活动。而且，他们能有一个好的团体，这很好。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can I add -- no, no, can I add another bit of context that's related to 250?
paragraphs.cn: 我能补充 --  -- 不，不，我能补充一点与250周年相关的背景吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: In an era where DEI has been completely, not just DEI. When black American history has been carved out of the halls of the federal government with a -- with like a butcher knife, them doing this library on Juneteenth week is on purpose and is significant.
paragraphs.cn: 在一个DEI被彻底 --  -- 不仅仅是DEI --  -- 的时代，当美国黑人的历史像用屠刀一样被从联邦政府的大厅中剔除时，他们在六月节这一周建立这个图书馆是有意为之且意义重大的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Because maybe for black Americans --
paragraphs.cn: 因为也许对美国黑人来说 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That is a historic moment.
paragraphs.cn: 那是一个历史性的时刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That this nation will no longer celebrate under the Trump administration. They are not interested in talking about the history of slavery. So, it feels like the Obamas are doing something very purposeful. They're creating an alternative, historical celebration for people who feel like part of their history is forgotten.
paragraphs.cn: 这个国家在特朗普政府下将不再庆祝。他们对谈论奴隶制历史不感兴趣。所以，感觉奥巴马夫妇在做一件非常有意义的事情。他们为那些觉得自己的历史被遗忘的人们创造了一个替代性的历史庆典。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: I heard an interview with Valerie Jarrett last night where she said that this is a place for hope to live on. And I think that that sentiment rings true, probably not only to black Americans, but all Americans who believe that we are living in chaos and just don't agree with the way that Trump goes about with the slashing of DEI and just the complete disregard for the majority of the American people who are not in his billionaire class.
paragraphs.cn: 昨晚我听到瓦莱丽·贾勒特的一次采访，她说这是一个让希望延续的地方。我认为这种情感是真实的，可能不仅对美国黑人，而且对所有相信我们生活在混乱中、不认同特朗普削减DEI以及完全无视不属于他亿万富翁阶层的绝大多数美国人的方式的人来说都是如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But I'm just adding, it's also historical.
paragraphs.cn: 但我只是补充一下，这也是历史性的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Like, this is also a historical week.
paragraphs.cn: 比如，这也是一个具有历史意义的一周。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: But I think that this is why you have so many people who are doing these wonderful documentaries and all these artists coming out.
paragraphs.cn: 但我认为，这就是为什么有这么多人制作这些精彩的纪录片，以及所有这些艺术家涌现出来的原因。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: So, I think it's more than just the pop culture aspect. I do think that we are in a place where a lot of people do not want to celebrate where we are at in our country's history. And I think that that's unfortunate. And I'm glad that a lot of people can go celebrate the Obamas, which is well deserved. He was a president for eight years. Everyone should go celebrate that. And they have -- it's a -- I worked in the Obama administration (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我认为这不仅仅是流行文化方面的问题。我确实认为，我们正处于一个很多人不想庆祝我们国家历史现状的时期。我认为这很不幸。我很高兴很多人能够去庆祝奥巴马夫妇，这是他们应得的。他担任了八年总统。每个人都应该去庆祝这一点。而且他们 --  -- 我在奥巴马政府工作过（听不清）。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. It's also a weird week because we're coming off that UFC fight on Sunday, where that fighter made essentially a cruel --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。这也是奇怪的一周，因为我们刚刚经历了周日的那场UFC比赛，那名选手基本上发表了一个残忍的 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: But it just goes to show you, priority.
paragraphs.cn: 但这恰恰向你展示了，优先级。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No, no, a cruel remark about Michelle Obama, our first lady.
paragraphs.cn: 不，不，是关于我们的第一夫人米歇尔·奥巴马的一句残忍言论。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, for her to be able to stand up on stage --
paragraphs.cn: 所以，她能够站在舞台上 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Undiminished, I think, is also a significant moment.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为，毫发无损，也是一个重要的时刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Look, I would love for us to be celebrating both the opening of the Obama library and the historic 250th anniversary of our democracy. I would -- I think that would be wonderful. And I would love for the artists that canceled on the America 250 not to have done that and to see beyond whatever partisan politics are happening now in this -- in this time.
paragraphs.cn: 听着，我很希望我们既能庆祝奥巴马图书馆的开放，也能庆祝我们民主制度建立250周年的历史性时刻。我希望 --  -- 我认为那会很棒。我也希望那些取消参加"美国250"活动的艺术家们没有那样做，并且能够超越当下任何党派政治的纷争。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Do you think they would have done that if it was the America 250 bipartisan organization that had been approved by Congress versus Freedom 250, the Trump administration's secondary organization that people have accused of politicizing the 250 event?
paragraphs.cn: 你认为，如果"美国250"是国会批准的两党组织，而不是特朗普政府被指责将250周年活动政治化的次级组织"自由250"，他们还会那样做吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: So, I'm not exactly sure people know the difference and the nuances.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我不太确定人们是否了解其中的区别和细微差别。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But the artists knew because they could back out.
paragraphs.cn: 但艺术家们知道，因为他们可以退出。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: But I feel that's like a very like D.C. bubble, nuanced conversation. But I do think that people don't want to be up on stage with Donald Trump, who you never know what he's going to say, you don't know what his cabinet is going to say, you don't know what the people who support him are going to say, and they run the risk.
paragraphs.cn: 但我觉得这很像华盛顿圈子里那种微妙的对话。不过，我确实认为人们不想和唐纳德·特朗普同台，因为你永远不知道他会说什么，不知道他的内阁会说什么，不知道支持他的人会说什么，他们冒着风险。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. But, I mean, Martina McBride said, this is more political than we thought it would be.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但我的意思是，玛蒂娜·麦克布莱德说，这比我们想象的要更具政治性。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, that was very clear.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，这一点非常清楚。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: And they don't know what he's going to say. And they don't believe in what he's saying. And I -- just like with most of the country doesn't believe in what he has to say. So, they don't want to be there supporting it.
paragraphs.cn: 而且他们不知道他会说什么。他们也不相信他说的话。我 --  -- 就像大多数美国人不相信他所说的话一样。所以，他们不想在那里支持他。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Well, in the meantime, for the people who might want to watch the opening of this library, CNN is doing massive, special live coverage of the event. It's going to start at 11 a.m. Eastern. You can watch that here. 11:30 Eastern. And you can watch that here or on the CNN app. Next on CNN THIS MORNING, Vice President Vance has become the face of the Iran deal. So, is Vance becoming the closer or the fall guy? Plus.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。与此同时，对于那些可能想观看这个图书馆开幕典礼的人，CNN正在进行大规模的特别直播报道。东部时间上午11点开始。你可以在这里观看。东部时间11点30分。你可以在这里或CNN应用程序上观看。接下来在CNN《今晨》节目中，副总统万斯已成为伊朗协议的代表人物。那么，万斯是成为终结者还是替罪羊？另外。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: And I've known him for 30 -- for 30 years, and I'm very proud to bring the team to the White House.
paragraphs.cn: 我认识他30年了，我很自豪能带领团队来到白宫。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, Knicks owner James Dolan says he is going to the White House. The big question is whether the team will follow.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，尼克斯队老板詹姆斯·多兰说他要去白宫。大问题是球队是否会跟随。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: If it works, then I'm going to take the credit. If it doesn't work out, I'm blaming J.D. You better be careful, J.D. He's going to turn his plane around and get the hell out of here. Yes, I like that idea.
paragraphs.cn: 如果成功了，我就把功劳揽下来。如果没成功，我就怪J.D.。你最好小心点，J.D.。他会掉转飞机，赶紧离开这里。是的，我喜欢这个主意。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, that is President Trump saying essentially that J.D. Vance is the one to blame for whatever comes next after the proposed Iran deal. And conservative voices online agree. Erick Erickson writing this, quote, "I see some people screaming about impeachment of the president over this deal. All that would do would be to put the architect of the deal into the Oval Office." Now, Vance, as we said, has become the face of at least selling this deal to the American people.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，特朗普总统基本上是在说，如果伊朗协议提出后发生任何事情，J.D.万斯应该受到指责。保守派的声音在网上也同意这一点。埃里克·埃里克森写道："我看到有些人因为这项协议而高呼弹劾总统。那样做只会把协议的策划者送进椭圆形办公室。"正如我们所说，万斯至少已成为向美国人民推销这项协议的代表人物。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: He's been everywhere this week, countless shows, podcasts. He's talking about his new book, "Communion," but also talking a lot about the proposal with Iran. Now, the person we haven't heard from quite as much, Marco Rubio, but he's been by the president's side in France for the G7 Summit. He even handed Trump the document to sign. So, how does this factor into, you know, a race for a potential MAGA successor? Group chat is back. I wanted to talk about this because there was so much made of J.D.
paragraphs.cn: 他这周到处露面，参加了无数节目和播客。他在谈论他的新书《圣餐》，但也谈了很多关于与伊朗的提议。现在，我们不太常听到马可·鲁比奥的消息，但他一直陪伴在总统身边，在法国参加七国集团峰会。他甚至递给特朗普签署的文件。那么，这如何影响潜在的MAGA接班人的竞争呢？群聊又回来了。我想谈谈这个，因为关于J.D.万斯代表党内孤立派声音的角色有很多讨论。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Vance's role representing the voice of the isolationist wing of the party. And so, I guess the White House solution has been to make him not. To make him the face of something else. Here is J.D. Vance with Megyn Kelly. Go into the belly of the beast, right, to have a conversation about this. And she really put to him a lot of questions. But here's an example of something he talked about with her.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我想白宫的解决方案是让他不再代表那个角色。让他成为其他事物的代表。这是J.D.万斯与梅根·凯利的对话。深入虎穴，对吧，来讨论这个问题。她确实问了他很多问题。但这是他跟她谈到的一个例子。

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.text: I just don't think that people who are criticizing this, one, they're not actually dealing with the reality of what's in it. And number two, they don't have an alternative. The benefits of the bargain only accrue again if Iran actually complies. We're not talking about rewarding words, we're talking about changing the way that we deal with Iran based on their actions. We've cut what is fundamentally a good deal for the American people, assuming the Iranians follow through.
paragraphs.cn: 我只是认为，那些批评这项协议的人，第一，他们实际上没有处理协议中的现实内容。第二，他们没有替代方案。只有伊朗真正遵守协议，协议的好处才会再次累积。我们不是在谈论奖励言辞，我们是在谈论根据伊朗的行动改变我们与伊朗打交道的方式。我们达成了一项对美国人民来说基本上是好协议，前提是伊朗人遵守承诺。

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.text: And if they don't follow through, there's no skin off our back. Nothing changes from where we are right now.
paragraphs.cn: 如果他们不遵守，我们也没什么损失。现状没有任何改变。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Sorry about that. That was him on a bunch of different shows. But what's interesting is, usually the interviewer is saying, this person is responding badly, this person is responding badly, and they're talking about Republicans. I'm not seeing Vance spending a lot of time beating back Democratic criticism.
paragraphs.cn: 抱歉。那是他在多个不同节目上的发言。但有趣的是，通常采访者会说，这个人反应不好，那个人反应不好，他们谈论的是共和党人。我没有看到万斯花很多时间回击民主党的批评。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: So, timing is everything in politics. And the vice president had the unfortunate timing issue of releasing a book and booking -- pre-booking all of these shows prior to the memorandum of understanding that got signed or didn't get signed on Sunday. I can't remember where we are with that one right now. So, that's part of the problem for him. He was set up for these.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，在政治中时机决定一切。副总统遇到了不幸的时机问题：在周日签署或未签署的谅解备忘录之前，他发布了一本书并预先预订了所有这些节目。我不记得现在那个协议进展如何了。所以，这是他面临的部分问题。他早就安排好了这些节目。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But he is talking about -- you know, he's the czar of fraud. I didn't hear about that.
paragraphs.cn: 但他正在谈论 --  -- 你知道，他是欺诈沙皇。我没听说过这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: He could have been, except that he's probably responding to the questions that he's getting from responsible reporters who have the vice president of the United States on their stage, pre-booked for his book tour and asking him questions --
paragraphs.cn: 他本可以是，但他可能只是在回答那些负责任的记者提出的问题，这些记者让美国副总统登上他们的舞台，预先安排了他的新书巡回宣传，并向他提问 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And because the administration has said he helped make the deal.
paragraphs.cn: 因为政府说他帮助达成了协议。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Yes. It was very interesting yesterday. And I watched the full press conference with the president as Peter Doocy was the one that asked that question about J.D. Vance.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。昨天非常有趣。我全程观看了总统的新闻发布会，彼得·杜西问了那个关于J.D.万斯的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: That was his second question. And I felt like there was a little bit of a setup there.
paragraphs.cn: 那是他的第二个问题。我觉得这有点像是事先安排好的。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Like, why did the president go back to Peter --
paragraphs.cn: 比如，为什么总统要回头找彼得 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: At the very end of the press conference to get that question? That was --
paragraphs.cn: 在新闻发布会快结束时才问那个问题？这 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, you're not beating the allegations here of saying that the White House is setting him up. Meghan, am I hearing something in this messaging?
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，你这么说可没法洗清白宫在给他下套的指控。梅根，我是不是听出了什么弦外之音？

paragraphs.speaker: I -- Hays
paragraphs.text: I mean, is anyone really going to think that J.D. Vance was really in charge of this? I mean, I think that --
paragraphs.cn: 我是说，真的会有人认为J.D.万斯负责了这一切吗？我觉得 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Oh, well, by the time this week is done, maybe.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，等这周结束，也许吧。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: I mean, I think that this is like --
paragraphs.cn: 我是说，这就像 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Well, that's the -- maybe the point.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，这也许就是关键。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: Are we just trying to hide that Jared Kushner was actually the one negotiating this deal, who does not work for the United States government? But, I mean, I just think that maybe Marco Rubio will end up better when he's trying to run for president. But I don't -- I don't think that Donald Trump cares who gets blamed as long as it's not him. But I don't think the American people are that stupid to realize their gas prices went up because we went into a war of choice by Donald Trump.
paragraphs.cn: 我们是不是在试图掩盖其实是贾里德·库什纳在谈判这笔交易，而他并不为美国政府工作？但我觉得，也许马可·鲁比奥在竞选总统时会更好。不过我不认为唐纳德·特朗普在乎谁被指责，只要不是他就行。但我不认为美国人民会蠢到意识不到他们的油价上涨是因为我们卷入了唐纳德·特朗普发起的非必要战争。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Does that make a difference? As she said, we didn't see anyone connected to Trump. The people he really trusts. I'm just saying that in the way that, like, Rubio is not the one going out defending this over and over again. Jared Kushner, as you mentioned, not a paid government employee, happily sent all over the world to negotiate on behalf of the U.S., also not out talking about this. Is it just happenstance because J.D. Vance has a book? WU: Yes, it is kind of remarkable.
paragraphs.cn: 这有区别吗？正如她所说，我们没有看到任何与特朗普有关的人。那些他真正信任的人。我只是想说，就像鲁比奥并没有反复出面为此辩护一样。你提到的贾里德·库什纳，虽然不是受薪政府雇员，却被愉快地派往世界各地代表美国谈判，他也没有出来谈论这件事。只是因为J.D.万斯有本书吗？吴：是的，这确实有点引人注目。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I mean the vice president, in many ways it would be deputized to carry out the presidents message, right? This is, you know, the president's agenda.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，副总统在很多方面会被授权传达总统的信息，对吧？这就是总统的议程。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So you're saying, this is the gig? WU: This is the gig.
paragraphs.cn: 所以你是说，这就是任务？吴：这就是任务。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. WU: This is the, you know, the job you get handed, whether you want it or not. We see this with vice presidents all the time. And for Vance it's extremely high risk.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。吴：这就是你接到的任务，不管你想不想做。我们经常在副总统身上看到这一点。对万斯来说，这风险极高。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Right. Kamala being border czar. WU: Exactly.
paragraphs.cn: 对。就像哈里斯担任边境事务主管。吴：没错。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. WU: Like she got, you know, she famously talked about how she didn't really want that. And yet, you know, she got that name. And so, with Vance, it's very high risk, high reward here. If they manage to actually pull this off, you know, he can settle a lot of the critics on the right who, you know, see him as maybe not as much in Trump's mold. And if he fails, you know, that that could also very much harm his chances in the future.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。吴：就像她，你知道，她曾著名地谈论过她并不真的想要那个职位。然而，她还是得到了那个名号。所以对万斯来说，这是高风险高回报。如果他们真的成功做到了，他就能平息许多右翼批评者，这些人可能认为他不那么符合特朗普的模式。而如果他失败了，那也可能严重损害他未来的机会。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. I want to talk about something else now because basically the Knicks were invited to be at the White House, ticker tape parade today. We're going to be talking about all the fans jamming the city streets. But after that, that trip to the White House, it's kind of a question mark. Now, Knicks owner, James Dolan, he's a longtime Trump supporter. He's confirmed that the president has issued a formal invitation.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。现在我想谈谈别的事情，因为尼克斯队今天被邀请到白宫参加彩带游行。我们将讨论所有挤满城市街道的球迷。但之后，去白宫的那趟行程，还是个问号。现在，尼克斯队老板詹姆斯·多兰，他是特朗普的长期支持者。他确认总统已经发出了正式邀请。

paragraphs.speaker: James Dolan
paragraphs.text: We just did receive an invitation from --
paragraphs.cn: 我们刚刚收到了一份邀请 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dolan
paragraphs.text: From the White House, which we accepted. We still have to figure out the details, et cetera, but, you know -- yes, of course. I mean, you know, look, I invited the president to come --
paragraphs.cn: 来自白宫的邀请，我们接受了。我们还需要确定细节等等，但是，你知道 --  -- 是的，当然。我的意思是，你看，我邀请了总统来 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dolan
paragraphs.text: To come down for the game, right? He is a friend. I've known him for 30 -- for 30 years. And I'm very proud to bring the team to the White House.
paragraphs.cn: 来看比赛，对吧？他是我的朋友。我认识他30年了。我很自豪能带球队去白宫。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, it's not clear whether all the Knicks players are going to be there, and it's not unusual for NBA players to boycott the White House. The Golden State Warriors did it following their 2017 and 2018 NBA championships. The group chat is back. This is such a week for politics and sports, you know what I mean? We started with this like octagon on the front lawn.
paragraphs.cn: 目前尚不清楚尼克斯队的所有球员是否都会出席，NBA球员抵制白宫之行并不罕见。金州勇士队在2017年和2018年赢得NBA总冠军后就这么做了。群聊又回来了。这周真是政治与体育交织的一周，你懂我的意思吗？我们从白宫草坪上的那个八角形开始说起。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We're -- and now, so here's the thing. You can do this kind of thing. It depends on where your fans are, right? It's not Kansas City, more red base NFL. It's not NHL, players from other countries, cool to go to the White House. It's the NBA, which basically Trump has had an antagonistic relationship with black athletes for a long time. So, do you think the Knicks are going to show to this?
paragraphs.cn: 现在，事情是这样的。你可以做这种事，这取决于你的球迷在哪里，对吧？这不是堪萨斯城，那里是NFL的红色根据地。这不是NHL，球员来自其他国家，去白宫很酷。这是NBA，特朗普基本上与黑人运动员长期处于敌对关系。那么，你认为尼克斯队会出席吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: I hope so. I mean, this, for me, this goes back to what I was saying about the presidential libraries. I think they're a great celebration for all Americans. I think going to the White House as a champion of your league is a great tradition that we have in this country. And so, I'm hopeful that everyone looks beyond whatever their partisan politics may be at the time and they go to the White House for that celebration.
paragraphs.cn: 我希望如此。我的意思是，对我来说，这又回到了我之前关于总统图书馆的说法。我认为它们是对所有美国人的伟大庆祝。我认为作为联盟冠军去白宫是我们国家的一个伟大传统。所以，我希望每个人都能超越当时的党派政治，去白宫参加庆祝活动。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Meghan, I want you to jump in. But, Mike, can you just answer one extra question I have to ask, just because you've worked in this White House. No, you've worked in this White House.
paragraphs.cn: 梅根，我想请你加入讨论。但是，迈克，你能回答我一个额外的问题吗？因为你在这届白宫工作过。不，你在这届白宫工作过。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Is any Trump event a nonpolitical event? That has not proven to be the case, to my mind, just covering --
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普的任何活动都是非政治性的吗？据我所知，情况并非如此，仅仅报道 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: You're asking this question of a Bills' fan who protested the Patriots coming to the White House when I was there. I took as many tickets as I could so that there were empty seats for the celebration of that team.
paragraphs.cn: 你是在问一个比尔队的球迷，他曾在我在白宫时抗议爱国者队来访。我尽可能多地拿了门票，这样庆祝那支球队时就会有空座位。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Yes. Not the (INAUDIBLE) you think it is.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。是的。不是你想的那样（听不清）。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: So, I also have been playing partisan politics with teams coming to the White House.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我也一直在对来访白宫的球队玩弄党派政治。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Actually terrifying. And I will have the Boston fans -- let's just zoom in on Mike's face for a second there. Let's just be absolutely clear. This is Mike.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。实际上很可怕。我会让波士顿的球迷 --  -- 让我们先放大迈克的脸。让我们绝对清楚。这是迈克。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: You can send me all the hate mail you want, Boston. Go for it because --
paragraphs.cn: 你可以给我寄所有你想寄的仇恨邮件，波士顿。来吧，因为 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. The other thing, Donald Trump and James Dolan, who's the owner of Madison Square Garden, they are collaborating on an $8 billion redevelopment deal of Penn Station. As always, there's a financial relationship.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。另一件事是，唐纳德·特朗普和麦迪逊广场花园的老板詹姆斯·多兰正在合作一个80亿美元的宾夕法尼亚车站重建项目。一如既往，这其中存在财务关系。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, yes, Dolan says they are friends. And I'm not saying Penn Station doesn't need the overhaul. But this design, of course, includes a presidential seal and Trump's name etched on an interior wall.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，是的，多兰说他们是朋友。我并不是说宾夕法尼亚车站不需要彻底翻新。但这个设计当然包括总统印章和特朗普的名字刻在内墙上。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: Yes, of course. I mean this is always going to -- there's always some sort of side hustle going on with the Trumps. But I also think what the Knicks players are going to do is look at what happened with the Giants when the quarterback came and introduced Trump and the backlash that he got and the controversy it caused within the team.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，当然。我的意思是，这总是会 --  -- 特朗普家族总是会有某种副业。但我也认为尼克斯球员会看看巨人队之前发生的事情，当时四分卫介绍特朗普，他遭到了强烈反对，并在球队内部引起了争议。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: And I think they're going to look long and hard whether or not they want to be placed in that situation because of whatever Donald Trump could or could not say at their celebration and think if they want to be made the puppets. I don't know that -- I don't know what their political views are. I have no idea. But I do think that we just saw this with the Giants in New York, and the Knicks are going to look long and hard if they want to be a part of it.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为他们会仔细考虑是否愿意被置于那种境地，因为唐纳德·特朗普在他们的庆祝活动上可能会说或不会说什么，并思考他们是否想成为傀儡。我不知道 --  -- 我不知道他们的政治观点。我完全不知道。但我确实认为我们刚刚在纽约巨人队身上看到了这一点，尼克斯队会仔细考虑是否想参与其中。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And it's also complicated by Trump showing up at that game.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。而且特朗普出现在那场比赛也让事情变得复杂。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And incensing the entire Knicks nation all at once. So, it's almost not a typical conversation.
paragraphs.cn: 并且一下子激怒了整个尼克斯国度。所以，这几乎不是一次普通的对话。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You have a president who has a deep relationship with New York, who has actually legitimately pissed off the entire city. And so, the Knicks, I don't know if they'll -- would really pay a price for not showing up. WU: Probably not. I mean the -- look at the celebrations that we're going to see today with the parade.
paragraphs.cn: 你有一位与纽约关系深厚的总统，他实际上已经激怒了整个城市。所以，尼克斯队，我不知道他们是否会 --  -- 真的为不出现付出代价。吴：可能不会。我的意思是，看看我们今天在游行中会看到的庆祝活动。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let me show -- let me show. WU: Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 让我展示 --  -- 让我展示。吴：好的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Even "The New York Post" has managed to say something nice. It's the "Knick York Post." I see what you did there. It feels like people are loving them too much. Can they do no wrong, though? WU: Yes. I mean, I think, you know, your average sports fan probably doesn't pay that much attention to exactly which player shows up at the White House and, you know, how that all goes down. So, you know, this might not be (INAUDIBLE) for them.
paragraphs.cn: 就连《纽约邮报》也设法说了些好话。这是"尼克斯邮报"。我明白你的意思。感觉人们太爱他们了。不过，他们能不出错吗？吴：是的。我的意思是，我认为，你知道，普通体育迷可能不太关注具体哪个球员出现在白宫，以及事情是如何进行的。所以，你知道，这对他们来说可能并不重要。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, look at how many people are showing up for the parade. I mean, they're into it. Well, one thing I want to say before we get to group chats is, thanks to the players union, they are not required, nor can they be compelled, to go to events that are political in nature. So, they do have a choice and Dolan cannot force them.
